content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Persisted SIGTERM handoff lease metadata so interrupted runs can reclaim a dead CLI writer lease even after `run.pid` is removed, added a repair path that appends a missing terminal `run_failed` event for signal-handoff failed snapshots during milestone sync, and hardened stale backend PGID cleanup so forced stop only targets orphaned backend groups when PID reuse has been ruled out while refusing reused live PIDs. Added regression coverage for dead-lease reclaim, signal-handoff journal repair, safe PGID refusal, and preserved stale-stop recovery when an authoritative backend leader has already exited with no reused PID.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Inspect the SIGTERM handoff, failed-snapshot sync, CLI writer lease reclaim, and stale backend cleanup paths to identify where durable recovery and PGID safety were still missing.
2. [Completed] Extend CLI writer leases with persisted cleanup-handoff process identity, write that handoff during termination-signal cleanup before `run.pid` removal, and teach failed/paused recovery to reclaim dead CLI cleanup leases without relying on `run.pid`.
3. [Completed] Add a signal-handoff repair path that appends a missing `run_failed` event for interrupted failed snapshots during milestone sync, and tighten stale backend PGID cleanup so orphan-group SIGKILL only happens when no live PID reuse is present.
4. [Completed] Add targeted regressions for dead-lease reclaim, signal-handoff journal repair, safe backend PGID refusal, and stale-stop recovery with an already-exited authoritative backend leader, then rerun formatting and the acceptance-command suite.

## Validation Evidence

- Targeted regression passed: `cargo test observed_cli_cleanup_owner_has_dead_pid_uses_persisted_cleanup_handoff_without_run_pid -- --nocapture`.
- Targeted regression passed: `cargo test sync_terminal_milestone_task_repairs_signal_handoff_failed_snapshot_without_run_failed_event -- --nocapture`.
- Targeted regression passed: `cargo test kill_stale_backend_process_group_refuses_exited_authoritative_leader_without_safe_proof -- --nocapture`.
- Adjacent stale-stop regression passed: `cargo test run_stop_recovers_stale_running_when_persisted_backend_leader_already_exited --test cli -- --nocapture`.
- Full suite passed: `cargo test`.
- Lint suite passed with pre-existing warnings only: `cargo clippy`.
- Formatting check passed: `cargo fmt --check`.

## Outstanding Risks

- `cargo clippy` still reports pre-existing warnings outside this change set (for example `dead_code`, `ptr_arg`, `large_enum_variant`, `too_many_arguments`, and related style warnings).
- The Unix backend process-group path was exercised on this Linux host; the shared non-Linux Unix implementation was not validated end-to-end in this pass.
- If a recorded backend PID has already been reused by a different live process, stale backend group cleanup now refuses to signal that PGID to avoid collateral damage; that rare case still requires manual cleanup of any surviving orphaned descendants.
